---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 500f49abddd684307b4e10c6951066e60fe0f107
findings:
  - { id: judged-wm-judge-1, kind: judged, text: "dc-3's deleted-signal is local-only — a worktree's branch is 'deleted' when it no longer appears in the current refs/heads/design/* listing — but verdi-store-layout's ratified Garbage collection section defines the gc deleted-signal differently: a prune-aware `git fetch --prune` first, then deleted = the ref exists neither locally NOR on the remote. A design branch deleted locally but still alive on the remote (exactly the remote-only entry the parent's dc-5 keeps in the enumeration, rendered sealed) is reclaim-eligible under dc-3 while not 'deleted' under store-layout's ratified definition. dc-3's disclosed judgment call covers only the retention_days question, not the deleted-signal definition itself, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against verdi-store-layout for redefining the signal. (confidence 0.60)", target_ref: spec/verdi-store-layout }
  - { id: judged-wm-judge-2, kind: judged, text: "dc-3 opts managed worktrees out of derived.retention_days entirely (reclaim-eligible the instant the merged/deleted signal fires, zero grace period), while verdi-store-layout's ratified config comment describes retention_days generally as the 'gc horizon for merged/deleted refs'. The story discloses this as a judgment call in prose, but prose disclosure is not a declared exempts edge: if the ratified comment binds every merged/deleted-ref reclaim mechanism rather than only derived/\u003cref\u003e/ pruning, this story exempts itself from a store-layout decision with no edge recorded. (confidence 0.40)", target_ref: spec/verdi-store-layout }
  - { id: judged-wm-judge-3, kind: judged, text: "dc-5 flips cmd/verdi gc from out-of-v0-scope to implemented while delivering only the managed-worktree slice, but verdi-store-layout's Garbage collection section ratifies `verdi gc` as also pruning derived/\u003cref\u003e/ past retention_days and pruning stale layout/tree-hash cache entries. Shipping the ratified verb name with a strict subset of its ratified behavior is disclosed only in the command's runtime output, not via a declared exempts/supersedes edge against the spec that owns the verb's definition. (confidence 0.30)", target_ref: spec/verdi-store-layout }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/workbench-directory#dc-1, spec/workbench-directory#dc-2, spec/workbench-directory#dc-3, spec/workbench-directory#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-5, spec/worktree-manager#dc-1, spec/worktree-manager#dc-2, spec/worktree-manager#dc-3, spec/worktree-manager#dc-4, spec/worktree-manager#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:30cd19d39e7505eb4ff6c8570c95b736dc4c8c529c704b09ec6e5acad80f544e
integrity: sha256:6e9b66d78c712ca7f7d8ed7a1b00381715af058f9f124b4ea8d1c2f96020f1e3
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"wm-judge-1\",\"text\":\"dc-3's deleted-signal is local-only — a worktree's branch is 'deleted' when it no longer appears in the current refs/heads/design/* listing — but verdi-store-layout's ratified Garbage collection section defines the gc deleted-signal differently: a prune-aware `git fetch --prune` first, then deleted = the ref exists neither locally NOR on the remote. A design branch deleted locally but still alive on the remote (exactly the remote-only entry the parent's dc-5 keeps in the enumeration, rendered sealed) is reclaim-eligible under dc-3 while not 'deleted' under store-layout's ratified definition. dc-3's disclosed judgment call covers only the retention_days question, not the deleted-signal definition itself, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against verdi-store-layout for redefining the signal.\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/verdi-store-layout\"},{\"id\":\"wm-judge-2\",\"text\":\"dc-3 opts managed worktrees out of derived.retention_days entirely (reclaim-eligible the instant the merged/deleted signal fires, zero grace period), while verdi-store-layout's ratified config comment describes retention_days generally as the 'gc horizon for merged/deleted refs'. The story discloses this as a judgment call in prose, but prose disclosure is not a declared exempts edge: if the ratified comment binds every merged/deleted-ref reclaim mechanism rather than only derived/\u003cref\u003e/ pruning, this story exempts itself from a store-layout decision with no edge recorded.\",\"confidence\":0.4,\"target\":\"spec/verdi-store-layout\"},{\"id\":\"wm-judge-3\",\"text\":\"dc-5 flips cmd/verdi gc from out-of-v0-scope to implemented while delivering only the managed-worktree slice, but verdi-store-layout's Garbage collection section ratifies `verdi gc` as also pruning derived/\u003cref\u003e/ past retention_days and pruning stale layout/tree-hash cache entries. Shipping the ratified verb name with a strict subset of its ratified behavior is disclosed only in the command's runtime output, not via a declared exempts/supersedes edge against the spec that owns the verb's definition.\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/verdi-store-layout\"}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/worktree-manager@500f49abddd684307b4e10c6951066e60fe0f107], digest: sha256:30cd19d39e7505eb4ff6c8570c95b736dc4c8c529c704b09ec6e5acad80f544e, integrity: sha256:6e9b66d78c712ca7f7d8ed7a1b00381715af058f9f124b4ea8d1c2f96020f1e3 }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-wm-judge-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: dc-3's deleted-signal is local-only — a worktree's branch is 'deleted' when it no longer appears in the current refs/heads/design/* listing — but verdi-store-layout's ratified Garbage collection section defines the gc deleted-signal differently: a prune-aware `git fetch --prune` first, then deleted = the ref exists neither locally NOR on the remote. A design branch deleted locally but still alive on the remote (exactly the remote-only entry the parent's dc-5 keeps in the enumeration, rendered sealed) is reclaim-eligible under dc-3 while not 'deleted' under store-layout's ratified definition. dc-3's disclosed judgment call covers only the retention_days question, not the deleted-signal definition itself, and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against verdi-store-layout for redefining the signal. (confidence 0.60)
- **judged-wm-judge-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: dc-3 opts managed worktrees out of derived.retention_days entirely (reclaim-eligible the instant the merged/deleted signal fires, zero grace period), while verdi-store-layout's ratified config comment describes retention_days generally as the 'gc horizon for merged/deleted refs'. The story discloses this as a judgment call in prose, but prose disclosure is not a declared exempts edge: if the ratified comment binds every merged/deleted-ref reclaim mechanism rather than only derived/<ref>/ pruning, this story exempts itself from a store-layout decision with no edge recorded. (confidence 0.40)
- **judged-wm-judge-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: dc-5 flips cmd/verdi gc from out-of-v0-scope to implemented while delivering only the managed-worktree slice, but verdi-store-layout's Garbage collection section ratifies `verdi gc` as also pruning derived/<ref>/ past retention_days and pruning stale layout/tree-hash cache entries. Shipping the ratified verb name with a strict subset of its ratified behavior is disclosed only in the command's runtime output, not via a declared exempts/supersedes edge against the spec that owns the verb's definition. (confidence 0.30)
